label: human
text: A lever is a simple machine made from a rigid bar that turns around a fixed point called a fulcrum. If the fulcrum is placed near the load, a smaller effort can move a heavier object, though the effort must travel through a longer distance.

label: human
text: Evaporation occurs when molecules at the surface of a liquid gain enough energy to become a gas. This can happen below the boiling point because some molecules are moving faster than others, especially when heat or moving air is present.

label: human
text: In a fraction, the denominator names the number of equal parts in the whole, while the numerator tells how many of those parts are being considered. The value of the fraction depends on both numbers and on the size of the whole being divided.

label: human
text: A plant stem supports leaves and carries water from the roots to other parts of the plant. It also transports sugars made in the leaves, so damage to the stem can affect both the plant's structure and its supply of food.

label: human
text: When two objects rub together, friction acts in the direction opposite the motion. Friction can slow a moving object, produce heat, or help surfaces grip each other, as when a shoe presses against the ground during walking.

label: human
text: A paragraph usually develops one main idea. The first sentence may introduce the topic, the middle sentences add details or evidence, and the final sentence can close the thought or prepare the reader for the next idea.

label: human
text: Sound travels as a vibration through matter. In air, vibrating objects push nearby air particles back and forth, and those particles pass the motion along until the vibration reaches the ear.

label: human
text: A map scale shows the relationship between distance on the map and distance in the real world. If one inch on the map represents ten miles, then two inches represents twenty miles.

label: ai
text: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to make food from sunlight. The plant takes in carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil, then uses light energy to produce glucose. Oxygen is released as a byproduct of this process.

label: ai
text: The water cycle describes how water moves through Earth's systems. Water evaporates from oceans and lakes, condenses into clouds, falls as precipitation, and eventually returns to bodies of water through runoff or groundwater flow.

label: ai
text: A thesis statement tells the reader the main point of an essay. It should be specific enough to guide the argument and broad enough to connect the body paragraphs into one clear purpose.

label: ai
text: Multiplication can be understood as repeated addition. For example, four groups of three can be written as three plus three plus three plus three, which equals twelve.

label: ai
text: Gravity is the force that pulls objects toward one another. On Earth, gravity pulls objects toward the center of the planet, which is why dropped objects fall downward.

label: ai
text: The main difference between weather and climate is time scale. Weather describes short-term conditions such as rain or temperature today, while climate describes long-term patterns in a region.

label: ai
text: An ecosystem includes living organisms and the nonliving parts of their environment. Plants, animals, water, soil, and sunlight all interact within the ecosystem.

label: ai
text: To solve a two-step equation, undo the operations in reverse order. First remove addition or subtraction, then remove multiplication or division, while doing the same operation to both sides.
